title: palicourea cenepensis
intro: Palicourea cenepensis, also known as the "Malaria Tree", is a plant species native to the Amazon rainforest, belonging to the Rubiaceae family. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, with its leaves containing compounds that have shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimalarial activities. The health benefits of Palicourea cenepensis include its potential to treat fever, malaria, and other infections, as well as its use in traditional medicine to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues and digestive problems. The plant is typically prepared as a decoction or infusion, with the leaves being harvested and then boiled in water to create a tea that is consumed for its medicinal properties.
